title: Conservative pseudo-label adaptation with calibrated disagreement filtering
summary: Tabular self-training can help when unlabeled test rows are plentiful, but only if pseudo-labels are filtered by confidence and agreement.
rationale: Tabular self-training can help when unlabeled test rows are plentiful, but only if pseudo-labels are filtered by confidence and agreement. This competition has a large test set and high-cardinality entities where mild distribution adaptation may matter.
implementation_hint: Build diverse OOF base models, calibrate them on OOF folds, and pseudo-label only extreme-agreement test rows, e.g. very low/high probabilities with low rank variance across models. Retrain one additional model on train plus pseudo-labeled rows, then give it a small ensemble weight.
expected_effect: Potential small gain from adapting to unseen driver/race combinations in test without changing the main modeling pipeline.
risk: Most dangerous idea here: overconfident mistakes can poison training, so keep thresholds strict, use a small blend weight, and abort if CV proxy degrades.
